verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, the signal peptide directs it into the ER lumen, and it is secreted as a soluble zymogen (pro-hK2) that is subsequently activated extracellularly. There is no transmembrane domain, no GPI-anchor signal, and no documented stable association with a membrane-anchored partner that would tether it to the cell surface in a wash-resistant manner. Before finalizing 'no': (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress- or activation-induced membrane display; it remains secreted even in tumor cells. (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of surface anchoring in any lineage. (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein. (4) dual_localization — no documented PM pool; it is detected in seminal plasma, serum and prostatic fluid as a soluble protein. (5) stable_surface_attachment — no published surface biotinylation or flow cytometry data on intact cells showing wash-resistant surface retention. Therapeutic uses (e.g., hK2-targeted radioimmunotherapy) exploit the secreted/soluble pool or pMHC epitopes, not direct surface binding to the protein body. Verdict: secreted only.
